steps.prompt: Which word is the verb of the relative clause?

hints.humiliabitur: 'Humiliabitur' is the main clause verb, not the relative clause verb

hints.to leap out: 'To leap out' is 'exsilire' — 'exaltare' means 'to raise up'

hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'exaltet' — 'exaltat' ends in -at (1st conj. indicative)

hints.exeo, exire, exii, exitum: These are the parts of 'exire' (to go out)
hints.tollo, tollere, sustuli, sublatum: These are the parts of 'tollere' (to lift) — a classical synonym
hints.alo, alere, alui, altum: These are the parts of 'alere' (to nourish) — 'altum' looks similar but different verb

steps.prompt: What tense, mood, and voice is 'humiliabitur'?

hints.present passive indicative: Present passive would be 'humiliatur' — 'humiliabitur' has the future -bi-
hints.future active indicative: Future active would be 'humiliabit' — the -tur ending marks passive
hints.perfect passive indicative: Perfect passive would be 'humiliatus est' — 'humiliabitur' is future

hints.humo, humare, humavi, humatum: These are the parts of 'humare' (to bury)
hints.humeo, humere, —, —: These are the parts of 'humere' (to be moist)
hints.habeo, habere, habui, habitum: These are the parts of 'habere' (to have)

hints.nominative: The reflexive pronoun has no nominative — 'se' is accusative
hints.ablative: 'Se' can be ablative, but as direct object of 'exaltat' it is accusative
hints.dative: Dative reflexive would be 'sibi' — 'se' is accusative

steps.prompt: What is the theological structure of this saying?
steps.target: divine passive: God is the implied agent who will humble
choices: divine passive: God is the implied agent who will humble

hints.the subject humbles himself: The passive 'humiliabitur' indicates someone else acts — the divine passive implies God
hints.other people will humble him: While possible, the 'divine passive' (passivum divinum) is a common biblical construction implying God acts
hints.nature will humble him: The passive here is theological — it implies divine action, not natural process

translation: Everyone who exalts himself will be humbled
